examples.title: Round Robin Scheduling Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how Round Robin schedules threads using time quantum
examples.code:
ALGORITHM RoundRobin(threads[], quantum)
BEGIN
  readyQueue = initializeQueue(threads)
  currentTime = 0
  
  WHILE readyQueue is not empty DO
    thread = dequeue(readyQueue)
    
    IF thread.remainingTime > quantum THEN
      currentTime = currentTime + quantum
      thread.remainingTime = thread.remainingTime - quantum
      enqueue(readyQueue, thread)  // goes to back
      PRINT "Time", currentTime, ": Thread", thread.id, "running"
    ELSE
      currentTime = currentTime + thread.remainingTime
      thread.remainingTime = 0
      thread.completionTime = currentTime
      PRINT "Time", currentTime, ": Thread", thread.id, "completed"
    END IF
  END WHILE
  
  RETURN calculateAverages(threads)
END
examples.explanation: Simulates Round Robin by processing each thread for quantum time, moving to back if not complete. Shows circular queue behavior.

examples.title: SJF Scheduling Implementation
examples.description: Implements Shortest Job First non-preemptive scheduling
examples.code:
def sjf_scheduling(processes):
    """
    processes: list of tuples (pid, burst_time, arrival_time)
    Returns average waiting time and turnaround time
    """
    # Sort by burst time (then by arrival for ties)
    sorted_processes = sorted(processes, key=lambda x: (x[1], x[2]))
    
    n = len(sorted_processes)
    waiting_time = [0] * n
    turnaround_time = [0] * n
    
    # First process starts at its arrival time
    current_time = sorted_processes[0][2]
    
    for i, (pid, burst, arrival) in enumerate(sorted_processes):
        # Wait if process hasn't arrived yet
        if current_time < arrival:
            current_time = arrival
        
        waiting_time[i] = current_time - arrival
        current_time += burst
        turnaround_time[i] = current_time - arrival
    
    avg_waiting = sum(waiting_time) / n
    avg_turnaround = sum(turnaround_time) / n
    
    return avg_waiting, avg_turnaround

# Example usage
processes = [('P1', 6, 0), ('P2', 8, 1), ('P3', 7, 2), ('P4', 3, 3)]
avg_wait, avg_turnaround = sjf_scheduling(processes)
print(f"Average Waiting Time: {avg_wait}")
print(f"Average Turnaround Time: {avg_turnaround}")
examples.explanation: Implements non-preemptive SJF by sorting processes by burst time. Calculates waiting time (start time - arrival) and turnaround time (completion - arrival).

examples.title: Priority Scheduling with Aging
examples.description: Demonstrates priority scheduling that prevents starvation using aging
examples.explanation: Implements priority scheduling with aging mechanism. Aging increases priority of waiting threads over time, preventing starvation of low-priority threads.

examples.title: FCFS Scheduling Algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FCFS(threads[])
BEGIN
  readyQueue = createQueue()
  
  // Add all threads to queue in arrival order
  FOR each thread IN threads DO
    enqueue(readyQueue, thread)
  END FOR
  
  currentTime = 0
  totalWaitingTime = 0
  totalTurnaroundTime = 0
  n = number of threads
  
  WHILE readyQueue is not empty DO
    thread = dequeue(readyQueue)
    
    // If thread arrives later, jump to that time
    IF currentTime < thread.arrivalTime THEN
      currentTime = thread.arrivalTime
    END IF
    
    // Calculate waiting time
    waitingTime = currentTime - thread.arrivalTime
    totalWaitingTime = totalWaitingTime + waitingTime
    
    // Execute thread
    currentTime = currentTime + thread.burstTime
    turnaroundTime = currentTime - thread.arrivalTime
    totalTurnaroundTime = totalTurnaroundTime + turnaroundTime
    
    PRINT "Thread", thread.id, ": Waiting =", waitingTime, 
          ", Turnaround =", turnaroundTime
  END WHILE
  
  PRINT "Average Waiting Time:", totalWaitingTime / n
  PRINT "Average Turnaround Time:", totalTurnaroundTime / n
END
examples.explanation: FCFS executes threads in arrival order. Simple algorithm but can cause long waiting times for short threads behind long ones (convoy effect).
